comment_text:
In the having clause section 1:43:35 , there appears to be an error. After applying the "having" clause, it displays the maximum number of students from the cities, whereas your intention was to select only those with scores above 90.

To achieve the desired outcome, the correct SQL query should be as follows:

SELECT city, COUNT(rollno)
FROM student
WHERE marks > 90
GROUP BY city;

comment_text:
Thanks for this video: I have 5 years of experience in excel and for interview I was revising.
It was a great video for revision but for beginner I can say it is not in-depth because cell referencing and so many things just covered very fast. Offset is not covered, Array formula, Sumproduct and Indrect also not covered. PMT and other loan related things are not covered, goal seek etc. In index only told about 3 criteria but we can use index with 2 criteria and 4 criteria as well. Yes I can say it is a great video but if you want to select in interview you have to learn in depth. You can follow Satish Sir other videos or Innozant channel because I learned everything from there. But for revision I wanted to learn from different teacher  to get broader knowledge.

One new thing I learnt from here is connect your slicer with form controller check box. But not sure where to use this feature because in video it was placed in unreverent place.

One downside, When Satish Sir, hide header of slicer then don't tell how to select all cities data as there was only option to select one city at a time because header was hide.

One last thing he used Formula of Filter, Sort and Take to find top five scorer but it is not working in 2019 or below excel versions and in many corporates even they do not provide 2019 version. So I think. Sir should tell different way to find the desired output.

I did not skip a single minute of this video, then only I am saying if you want to learn each things from basics and different ways to solve same problem then you should give a chance to innozant channel.

=trim to remove spaces

=if(condition, result 1, result 2) in case results are in text use inverted commas and if in no. no need
ctrl+e for flash fill
if and ifs
AND / OR with IF F@2:30 hr
sort>add level

Key Chapters & Topics Covered:
Excel Basics (0:06:00): Understanding the interface, rows, columns, cell addresses, and formulas.
Data Entry & Basic Formulas (0:41:31): Fundamental formula application and data management techniques.
Data Cleaning (1:26:45): Crucial steps for cleaning datasets, including removing duplicates, clearing blank rows, handling leading/trailing spaces, and correcting text cases.
Logical Functions (2:10:32): Using IF, IFS, AND, and OR functions to build conditional logic.
Filter & Sort (2:32:02): Techniques for sorting and filtering data to extract insights.
Lookup Functions (2:58:35): Mastering VLOOKUP, MATCH, and INDEX for dynamic data retrieval.
Statistical & Date Formulas (3:46:01 - 4:16:00): Performing statistical analysis and managing date formats effectively.
Pivot Tables (4:34:25): Creating and customizing Pivot Tables for summarizing data.
Dashboard Project (5:03:10): A practical project to build an Excel dashboard, including techniques for formatting, hiding gridlines, and preparing the final output for presentation.
